data.title: Energy Services of America launches public stock offering
data.description:
• Energy Services of America Corporation (ESOA) announced plans for an underwritten public offering of its common stock.
  • The company expects to grant the underwriter a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 15% of the shares sold.
  • Net proceeds are intended for general corporate purposes, working capital, and potential acquisitions.

data.title: Edison Non-GAAP EPS of $1.86 beats by $0.41

data.title: Top large-cap consumer discretionary stocks ranked by lowest RSI
data.description:
As we move into the second half of the quarter, below is a list of the top 10 large cap consumer stocks with market capitalizations of $10B or more. The list is ranked based on their lowest RSI.

The list is topped by Wayfair (W), with an RSI of 32. Genuine Parts Company (GPC) and Coupang (CPNG) are next, with Carvana (CVNA) and NVR (NVR) rounding out the rest of the top five. Somnigroup International (SGI), Las Vegas Sands (LVS) and Best Buy (BBY) are among the top ranked stocks as well, each with an RSI of less than 50.

Service Corporation International (SCI) and Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) complete the list, with RSI values of 47 and 48 respectively.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period, typically 14 days.

RSI readings of 70 or above are generally considered a signal that a stock may be overbought and potentially poised for a pullback.

Here is the list:

  1. Wayfair (W), RSI: 32

  2. Genuine Parts Company (GPC), RSI: 33

  3. Coupang (CPNG), RSI: 34

  4. Carvana Co. (CVNA), RSI: 35

  5. NVR (NVR), RSI: 40

  6. Somnigroup International (SGI), RSI: 41

  7. Las Vegas Sands (LVS), RSI: 45

  8. Best Buy (BBY), RSI: 45

  9. Service Corporation International (SCI), RSI: 47

  10. Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG), RSI: 48

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI)

data.title: Booking Holdings GAAP EPS of $44.22 misses by $2.15, revenue of $6.35B beats by $220M
data.description:

  • Shares -1.64% AH.

data.title: Costamare rises to 11-year high after Q4 beat, adding charter agreements worth $940M
data.description:
Costamare (CMRE) +0.3% in Wednesday's trading after previously rising as high as $17.52, poised to post its best close since August 2014, after reporting Q4 adjusted earnings and revenues that beat analyst expectations.

Costamare (CMRE) said it locked in ~$940M in fresh contracted revenues after fixing 12 vessels on a forward basis for periods stretching up to eight years, boosting fleet employment to 96% for 2026 and 92% for 2027; as a result, the company's contracted revenue backlog now totals ~$3.4B, with a TEU-weighted remaining duration of 4.5 years as of mid-February.

The fixtures cover a broad range of tonnage: five 14,400 TEU-capacity vessels, minimum period of eight years; four 5,000 TEU-capacity vessels, minimum period of three years; two 9,400 TEU-capacity vessels, minimum period of three years; and one 4,200 TEU-capacity vessel, minimum period of three years.

Across all 12 ships, the TEU-weighted average duration is about six years, the company said.

Costamare (CMRE) also said it is moving forward with fleet renewal with a newbuilding program of six 3,100 TEU containerships at the Zhoushan Changhong shipyard, with deliveries scheduled during 2027-28, with the full series backed by eight-year time charters to a leading liner operator.

data.title: Travel+Leisure GAAP EPS of -$0.95 misses by $2.19, revenue of $1.03B beats by $50M
data.description:
• Travel+Leisure press release (TNL): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.95 misses by $2.19.
  • Revenue of $1.03B (+6.1% Y/Y) beats by $50M.

  • Full-year 2025 highlights:

    • Net revenue of $4.02 billion. Gross VOI sales of $2.49 billion, up 8% year-over-year, including 6% VPG growth and 3% Tour growth (1)
    • Net income of $230 million (diluted EPS of $3.44), inclusive of $216 million in inventory write-downs and impairments related to the Resort Optimization Initiative
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $990 million and Adjusted diluted EPS of $6.34(1)
    • Net cash provided by operating activities of $640 million and Adjusted free cash flow of $516 million (1)

    Outlook:

    • Full Year 2026 Adjusted EBITDA expected to range from $1,030 million to $1,055 million, including the positive net impact of the Resort Optimization Initiative
    • The Company will recommend increasing first quarter 2026 dividend to $0.60 per share for approval by the Board of Directors
    • The Company’s Board of Directors approved a new $750 million share repurchase authorization

data.title: ‘Wouldn’t rule out’ inflation reigniting due to tariffs, labor costs – Charles Schwab’s Liz Ann Sonders
data.description:
Liz Ann Sonders, chief investment strategist at Charles Schwab, warned that inflation could reignite in the coming months, potentially forcing the Federal Reserve to shift its policy stance.

“I wouldn’t rule it out,” Sonders said in an interview with CNBC, noting that some Fed members have already expressed support for rate hikes if price pressures return.

The strategist explained that many companies have delayed passing along tariff-related costs to consumers, in part because they built up inventory at lower prices during the 90-day delay on reciprocal tariffs.

Now, however, businesses are beginning to raise prices due to both increased tariff costs and rising labor expenses.

“I think there is a risk that inflation not necessarily reignites to a significant degree but ticks up enough that we have to change the outlook for Fed policy,” Sonders said.

On the question of interest rate cuts, Sonders expressed skepticism given the current economic conditions.

“I don’t think the backdrop merits rate cuts here,” she said, adding that it would not surprise her if the Fed took no action on rates this year. She noted that the central bank remains in “data dependency mode,” with significant dispersion among Fed officials on the appropriate policy path.

Sonders also addressed the ongoing rotation in equity markets away from technology stocks and toward cyclical sectors such as energy, materials, and industrials. She attributed this shift partly to the infrastructure demands of artificial intelligence, particularly the build-out of data centers and their significant energy requirements.

The strategist described the evolution of AI investment through what she called the “three Cs”: create, catalyze, and cascade.

The market has now entered the cascade phase, which is “whipping through markets really quickly as investors try to figure out not just who the beneficiaries are of AI, and that spans across industries and sectors and companies, but who is being disrupted,” she explained.

Sonders cautioned that current market dynamics reflect a “sell first, do real research later kind of backdrop,” driven by investors with extremely short time horizons. This environment has created rapid price swings as market participants scramble to identify both the winners and losers from AI-driven disruption across the economy.

data.title: Valaris racks up $900M in new rig awards and extensions, fleet status report says
data.description:
Valaris (VAL) +1.9% in Wednesday's trading after saying it secured nearly $900M worth of new contracts and contract extensions since late October, according to its latest fleet status report.

The company said its Valaris DS-7 drillship won a five-well contract extension with Azule Energy offshore Angola, which is expected to begin in October 2026 in direct continuation of the existing program and last 325 days, adding ~$125M to the contracted revenue backlog; the contract also includes a five-well unpriced option with an estimated duration of 300-350 days.

The Valaris DS-9 drillship, under hire by Exxon Mobil since 2022, was awarded a two-year contract extension by Exxon, which is expected to begin in July 2026 in direct continuation of the existing program at an operating dayrate in line with recent market rates in the region; the contract also includes two six-month options.

Valaris' (VAL) jackup fleet won several contracts, including an eight-well contract for the Valaris 106 jackup with BP in Indonesia, which is expected to begin in Q3 2026 and estimated to last two years, adding ~$74M to the contracted revenue backlog; the contract also includes four option wells.

The contract backlog from all new contracts, which also included the previously reported $300M deal for the Valaris DS-8, rose to ~$4.7B from $4.5B as of October 2025.

Last week, Transocean signed a definitive agreement to acquire Valaris (VAL) in an all-stock deal valued at ~$5.8, creating an offshore drilling fleet of 73 rigs, including 33 ultra-deepwater drillships, nine semisubmersibles and 31 modern jackups.

data.title: Perion targets 25% CAGR in Perion One spend through 2028 as AI infrastructure scales
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Perion Network Ltd. (PERI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Tal Jacobson described 2025 as "year 1 for the new Perion," stating, "We redefined our mission, our strategy, our technology, our organizational structure and even our executive team, all in 1 year." Jacobson highlighted the launch and expansion of Perion One, positioning it as "an AI-native execution infrastructure that delivers measurable results across channels, platforms and verticals." Outmax, the company's AI execution agent, is now operating in more channels and driving significant results for customers.

  • Jacobson presented examples of Outmax's impact, noting, "The Outmax AI algorithmic model started with this specific advertiser on a $50,000 YouTube budget test in 2023...we grew the spend to $4.5 million in 2024 and to $20 million in 2025." He also emphasized new partnerships, "This quarter, we launched 3 new partnerships with Amazon, Walmart and Mastercard."

  • CFO Elad Tzubery stated, "We delivered 19% year-over-year growth in contribution ex-TAC resulting from our go-to-market strategy. We achieved a 53% surge in adjusted EBITDA...and we generated over 400% year-over-year increase in operating cash flow."

Outlook

  • Jacobson announced 2028 targets: "Specifically, we are targeting Perion One pro forma spend CAGR of at least 25%, contribution ex-TAC CAGR of at least 20% and adjusted EBITDA margins reaching 28% of contribution ex-TAC."

  • For 2026, Tzubery provided guidance: "We expect contribution ex-TAC of $215 million to $235 million and adjusted EBITDA of $50 million to $54 million."

  • The outlook for Perion One is for it to comprise 85% to 90% of consolidated contribution ex-TAC starting in 2026.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q4 grew 6% year-over-year to $137.1 million. Contribution ex-TAC increased 19% year-over-year to $65.2 million. Adjusted EBITDA reached $24.3 million, a 53% increase compared to last year.

  • Cash generated from operating activities in Q4 was $21.8 million, with a year-end net cash position of $313 million even after repurchasing almost $24 million in shares in the fourth quarter.

  • For the full year 2025, revenue was $439.9 million, contribution ex-TAC was $203.4 million, and adjusted EBITDA reached $45.2 million. Annual cash from operations was $41.9 million, up 504% year-over-year.

  • CTV revenue grew 59% in Q4 and 42% for the full year to $62.1 million. Digital Out-of-Home revenue rose 28% in Q4 and 36% for the year to $94.9 million. Retail Media revenue increased 42% in Q4 and 36% for the year.

  • Advertising solutions revenue increased by 7% year-over-year in Q4. CTV and Digital Out-of-Home collectively made up 44% of Q4 revenue. Web declined 17% year-over-year in Q4. Search revenue increased by 3% in Q4.

  • Non-GAAP net income for Q4 was $21.4 million, or $0.49 per diluted share. For the full year, non-GAAP net income was $51.3 million, or $1.13 per diluted share.

Q&A

  • Andrew Marok, Raymond James: Asked about the wide 2026 guidance range and political impacts. CFO Tzubery responded that "guidance for 2026 represents the current view of the business. We expect to see a gradual decline in search and our other legacy activities, in parallel, a sharper increase in the Perion contribution ex-TAC."

  • Marok followed up on acceleration between 2026 and 2028. Tzubery explained that growth is primarily driven by the shift to Perion One and away from legacy search.

  • Eric Martinuzzi, Lake Street: Asked about Amazon DSP integration and wallet share gains. Jacobson said, "Amazon DSP...request actually came from our customers...we've been working with Amazon for quite a while on that integration, and we're extremely happy about it."

  • Martinuzzi also inquired about differentiation from legacy DSPs. Jacobson clarified that Perion One is designed "to be a layer above all of those DSPs and optimize cross channel...our execution infrastructure is built in a way where other AI agent can interact with our AI agent."

  • Jason Kreyer, Craig-Hallum: Asked about Outmax adoption. Jacobson reported, "We're seeing strong adoption...anything between 40% to almost 80% uplift with some of our advertisers."

  • Steven Hromin, Oppenheimer: Queried about confidence in achieving 28% EBITDA margin by 2028. Tzubery stated, "We took a lot of efficiency measures already this year and we will continue to invest in them, specifically around the G&A and the cost of revenues using automation and AI tools."

  • Laura Martin, Needham: Asked about web decline fundamentals. Tzubery explained, "The decline is driven from 2 different reasons. First...we shut down low tech and low-margin legacy activities...Second...human behavior is shifting towards otherworld gardens."

  • Jeff Martin, ROTH: Asked about market share gains and growth contributors. Tzubery highlighted, "CTV led with almost 60% year-over-year growth. Digital Out-of-Home with 28%. The Retail Media vertical once again increased 42% in Q4."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, focused on guidance clarity, product adoption, and market share gains. Questions probed assumptions underlying forecasts, competitive positioning, and sustainability of growth.

  • Management maintained a confident and occasionally promotional tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing execution and strategic partnerships. During Q&A, responses were detailed but at times cautious, especially regarding long-term assumptions and the evolving ad tech landscape.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts showed increased interest in forward projections and AI-driven growth, with analysts still pressing for detail on execution and sustainability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter demonstrated acceleration in core growth engines, with a 19% year-over-year increase in contribution ex-TAC versus 7% in Q3, and adjusted EBITDA up 53% from 63% in Q3, indicating sustained margin expansion.

  • Management's tone was more forward-looking, introducing explicit 2028 targets for Perion One and emphasizing the company's transformation into an AI-native execution infrastructure.

  • Analysts maintained a focus on guidance ranges, market share sustainability, and product adoption, with recurring questions on differentiation and margin drivers.

  • The company provided more granular financial and operational targets for 2028, marking a shift from previous quarters' focus on near-term execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the continued decline in web revenue due to human behavior shifts and the discontinuation of low-margin activities.

  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of rapid growth in CTV, Digital Out-of-Home, and Retail Media, as well as potential risks from reliance on AI-driven differentiation versus traditional DSPs.

  • Management addressed these challenges by emphasizing ongoing investments in AI tools, operational efficiency, and new partnership integrations to drive recurring revenue and market expansion.

Final Takeaway

Perion’s fourth quarter marked a pivotal shift as the company completed its transformation into an AI-native execution infrastructure. With the strong adoption of Perion One and Outmax, double-digit growth across core advertising channels, and new partnerships with Amazon, Walmart, and Mastercard, management has set ambitious 2028 targets, including a 25% CAGR in Perion One spend and expanding EBITDA margins. The focus remains on scaling innovation and efficiency, while navigating the transition away from legacy web and search activities to become a platform of choice for performance-driven advertisers.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
